categories.description: Emails related to flight bookings, flight cancellations, updates, or confirmations.
Emails about hotel, Airbnb or any type of accommodation bookings, confirmations, cancellations, or other related communications.
Emails concerning car rental bookings, pickup and drop-off details, or car rental confirmations.
Emails regarding travel insurance bookings, policies, updates, and claims.
Emails that relate to travel but don't fall into the other specific categories, such as tour bookings, visa confirmations or travel-related advice.

options.systemPromptTemplate: Please classify the text provided by the user into one of the following categories: {categories}. The content may be in English, Hebrew, or Turkish. Understand the meaning regardless of language and use the provided formatting instructions below. Don't explain, and only output the json.

categories.description: Emails containing billing statements, receipts, or invoices NOT related to travel bookings or trip arrangements. These include confirmations for product purchases, service payments, utility bills, subscriptions, consulting fees, or business-related reimbursements. Excludes receipts for flight tickets, hotel reservations, car rentals, travel insurance or any travel related services.

Emails related to educational content or materials. This includes lecture notes, Torah portions, study guides, academic research, online course materials, learning resources, and any other content meant for personal or professional development.

Emails from the rabbinate regarding religious duties, events, or responsibilities. This includes correspondence with other clergy, congregational communication, event invitations, or any email connected to religious roles and activities.

Emails related to travel plans, whether for personal or business trips. This includes flight bookings, hotel reservations, car rentals, travel insurance, and their associated receipts or confirmations. It may also include trip itineraries, confirmations, or other documents related to travel such as permits, visas, etc. If an email involves any kind of travel service, even if it's in the form of a receipt, it belongs here.

options.systemPromptTemplate: =Please classify the text provided by the user into one of the following categories: {categories}. The content may be in English, Hebrew, or Turkish. Understand the meaning regardless of language and use the provided formatting instructions below. Don't explain, and only output the json.

values.content:
=You are a travel information extraction expert. Your task is to analyze an email about travel (e.g., hotel, car rental, travel insurance) and extract the following information in a structured JSON format:

Extract and return the following fields:

- destination: string — the city and country this travel item relates to (e.g., "Paris, France").
- start_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD) — the earliest relevant date (e.g., departure, check-in, pickup).
- end_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD) — the latest relevant date (e.g., return, check-out, drop-off).
- type: string — type of travel info as one of the following: "hotel", "car", "travel insurance", or "other".
- details: string — brief summary of key information (e.g., "Car rental from Hertz in Istanbul, Turkey").
- confidence_score: number — a value from 0 to 1 indicating your confidence in the extracted information.
- status: string — the completion status of the trip item if determinable, one of: "incomplete", "booked", "pending", "rescheduled",  "canceled", or "not needed" (if indeterminable default to "incomplete").
- document_url: string — any confirmation, ticket, or receipt URL if found (else leave blank).
- email_id: string — a unique identifier for the email where this information was found (you may receive this as input).
- user_id: string — the user this email belongs to (you may receive this as input).

Return the extracted fields in the following JSON format:

{
  "destination": "City, Country",
  "start_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "end_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "type": "hotel" | "car" | "travel insurance" | "other",
  "details": "short summary",
  "confidence_score": 0.95,
  "status": "incomplete",
  "document_url": "",
  "email_id": "198520a817c65c4b",
  "user_id": "abc@example.com"
}

=You are a travel information extraction expert. Your task is to analyze an email about travel (e.g., flight, hotel, car rental, travel insurance) and extract the following information in a structured JSON format:

Extract and return the following fields:

- destination: string — the city and country this travel item relates to (e.g., "Paris, France").
- start_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD) — the earliest relevant date (e.g., departure, check-in, pickup).
- end_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD) — the latest relevant date (e.g., return, check-out, drop-off).
- type: string — type of travel info as one of the following: "flight", "hotel", "car", "travel insurance", or "other".
- details: string — brief summary of key information (e.g., "Round-trip flight from Toronto to Paris on Air Canada").
- confidence_score: number — a value from 0 to 1 indicating your confidence in the extracted information.
- status: string — the completion status of the trip item if determinable, one of: 
  - "booked" (if the booking of the item has been successfully completed),
  - "pending" (if the item is not booked yet and is pending completion),
  - "rescheduled"(if the previously booked item has had new changes made to it such 
    as change of schedule, dates, route, etc.),
  - "canceled" (if the previously booked item has been canceled),
  - "not needed" (if this trip item is not needed, will be a manual option to select 
    for the user in unquie sitations),
  - "incomplete" (default value, if indeterminable default to "incomplete"),
- document_url: string — any confirmation, ticket, or receipt URL if found (else leave blank).
- email_id: string — a unique identifier for the email where this information was found (you may receive this as input).
- user_id: string — the user this email belongs to (you may receive this as input).

Return the extracted fields in the following JSON format:

{
  "destination": "City, Country",
  "start_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "end_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "type": "flight" | "hotel" | "car" | "travel insurance" | "other",
  "details": "short summary",
  "confidence_score": 0.95,
  "status": "incomplete",
  "document_url": "",
  "email_id": "198520a817c65c4b",
  "user_id": "abc@example.com"
}

values.content:
=You are a receipt and invoice information extraction expert. Your task is to analyze an email containing a receipt or invoice and extract the following information in a structured JSON format.

Extract and return the following fields:

- vendor: string — the company or individual associated with the receipt (e.g., "Amazon", "Home Depot", "Rabbi Moshe Cohen").
- total_amount: string — the total amount paid. Always format with the currency symbol **before** the number, regardless of local style (e.g., "$75.99", "€120.00", "₪250").
- received_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD) — the date the receipt or invoice was received (e.g., the email's received date).
- expense_date: string (YYYY-MM-DD) — the actual date the expense or transaction occurred. If unclear, use the received_date.
- category: string — categorize the receipt into one of the following (if determinable):
  - "home"
  - "office"
  - "donation"
  - "utilities"
  - "education"
  - "medical"
  - "other"

- details: string — brief summary of what the receipt is for (e.g., "Office supplies from Staples", "Monthly donation to Yeshiva Ateret").
- confidence_score: number — a value between 0 and 1 indicating your confidence in the accuracy of the extracted data.
- document_url: string — a link to any attached invoice or receipt file if available, else leave blank.
- email_id: string — a unique identifier for the email this receipt came from (you may receive this as input).
- user_id: string — the user this email belongs to (you may receive this as input).

Return the extracted information in the following JSON format:

{
  "vendor": "Amazon",
  "total_amount": "$75.99",
  "received_date": "2025-06-10",
  "expense_date": "2025-06-09",
  "category": "home",
  "details": "Home supplies from Amazon",
  "confidence_score": 0.92,
  "document_url": "",
  "email_id": "CAA5qxOaHtcTqmhmg-wLi+q6PSkHHJi6PLg3mjZHpUpyNtJ612A@mail.gmail.com",
  "user_id": "abc@example.com"
}

workflowId.cachedResultName: Dev - Supabase Storage Receipt File Upload
